name: BEAMM non-oracle scope benchmark
purpose: Use BEAMM documentation as a coverage yardstick for Belgium tax-benefit surfaces. Do not use it as law or as an Axiom parity oracle.
household_level_oracle_requirement: A RuleSpec oracle must accept household-level inputs and return household-level outputs for the same case. BEAMM documentation and aggregate simulator outputs do not satisfy this requirement.
source_priority_note: Each benchmark item still requires upstream Belgian statute, ordinance, decree, regulation, or agency source discovery before RuleSpec encoding.

benchmark_surfaces.remaining_gap: Brussels/Flemish/Walloon encoded monthly family-benefit amount surfaces now have a Belgian-facing regional routing bridge and core child-entitlement gates; German-speaking Community core 2019 amount surfaces, the 2025-2027 indexation-freeze selector, the 2025 annual-supplement override, and the statutory base-entitlement gate are encoded with the same routing bridge and supplied current/pre-2025 index-factor interfaces; the federal guaranteed-family-benefits law and 1971 implementing royal decree are encoded for principal-maintenance, residence, other-benefit, resource-ceiling, claim-window, public-placement, birth/adoption, advance, and supplied amount interfaces; and the LGAF coordinated-law amount schedule is encoded for rank-based monthly amounts, selected supplements, age supplements, orphan amount, first/second birth grants, adoption premium, and Article 76bis indexation interfaces. Remaining gaps are exact current regional amount/index tables where not encoded from upstream instruments, the third-and-later birth-grant BEF-to-EUR conversion source, delegated regional proof/obstacle/education conditions, and executable household-level oracle parity.

Main Brussels, Flemish, and Walloon gift-tax rate schedules plus Belgian-facing regional routing are encoded, including the Walloon future 2028 immovable-gift table, Brussels article-131bis movable-gift exemption handling for qualifying prior gifts mentioned in a successoral pact or Civil Code article 4.84 agreement, and Brussels/Walloon movable-gift exclusions for donor-death condition or term cases assimilated to succession duty. Remaining gaps are other exemptions, reduced rates, filing rules, business-transfer regimes, and broader household transaction inputs.

Social-integration Article 3/4/7 top-level eligibility predicates, effective-residence definition, Article 14 base amounts, Article 15 supplied-index-factor bridge, Article 14 section 2 payable amount bridge with eligibility gate and aggregate-or-itemized Chapter II resource offset, Article 22 resource exclusions, Article 23/35 earned-income countability, Article 24 business-transfer treatment, Article 25-27 immovable-property, rent, and movable-capital valuation mechanics, Article 28-32 transfer-of-assets mechanics, Article 33 in-kind housing support, Article 34 spouse/de facto and single or aggregate adult-maintenance-debtor resource attribution, and earned-income disregards are encoded, as are GRAPA article-6 maximum amounts, article-7/article-11 resource-selection and offset mechanics, and an article-6/7/11 payable amount bridge after supplied resource exclusions and exemption inputs. Remaining gaps are CPAS equity/proof/procedure interfaces, delegated current social-integration index-factor source, and delegated GRAPA exclusions and exemption amounts.

Main Brussels, Flemish, and Walloon inheritance-tax rate schedules plus Belgian-facing regional routing are encoded, including the Walloon future 2028 succession-duty table. Remaining gaps are exemptions, partner-home exclusions, reduced rates, estate grouping, and household beneficiary inputs.

Article 17 top-level movable-income category aggregation, Article 18 dividend-component aggregation, general movable-withholding, and regulated savings-deposit exemption/taxable-interest calculations are encoded. Remaining gaps are special instrument inclusions/exemptions and rates, copyright-income limits, net movable-income integration, withholding reconciliation, and household disposable-income integration.

Article 30/33 admission thresholds over supplied workday and half-day aggregates, Article 110 household-status classification, Article 111 average daily-wage caps, Article 114 ordinary unemployment duration/rate/degressivity selectors, Article 114 temporary-unemployment replacement-rate selectors, Article 116 non-running and extension-event selectors, Article 115 minimum daily unemployment amounts, Article 131ter youth/senior holiday allowance daily amounts, and ordinary and temporary monthly payable-amount bridges are encoded; the ordinary payable bridge uses Article 114 when no replacement-rate override is supplied, Article 115 can derive household status from Article 110 when the status-code override is zero, and Article 116 readmission can use Articles 30/33 when no explicit admission flag is supplied. Remaining gaps are full employment-history reconstruction, delegated household-status definitions and proof, Article 116 calendar-date recomputation, temporary-unemployment payment-calendar details, sanctions, activation interfaces, and household-level oracle parity.

Article 32 item 3/5 status bridging, Article 86/112 holder-status bridging, Article 131 thirty-day continuity, Article 114/114bis protection-period mechanics, delegated Article 216/217/219bis/222 maternity-indemnity rate interfaces, and Article 203/1, 205/1, 206/1, and 207/1 waiting-stage and continuing-entitlement numeric predicates are encoded from supplied remuneration, status, contribution, and compensable-day inputs. Remaining gaps are wage-cap/current indexed daily maxima, average-daily-wage calculation, unemployment phase routing, payment procedure, broader Article 32 categories and Article 131 date reconstruction, and broader sickness-insurance interactions.

Federal disability-allowance statutory amount/category surfaces are encoded, and Brussels APA is encoded for residence/incompatibility gates, the federal age-threshold bridge, autonomy category selection, statutory base annual amounts, supplied index-factor bridge, household income exemptions, selected pension/property/capital/deemed-transfer income mechanics, minimum annual payment, detention/social-defense suspension, and annual/monthly award amounts. Remaining gaps are delegated current index-factor tables, medical-guide evidence scoring, application/proof/procedure, other regional/community care supports, and executable household-level oracle parity.

GRAPA article-6 maximum amounts, article-7/article-11 resource-selection and offset mechanics, and an article-6/7/11 payable amount bridge are encoded. Worker retirement-pension legal age, current early-retirement thresholds, Article 5 60 percent and 75 percent remuneration fractions, Article 7 survivor-pension interfaces, Article 7bis transition allowance, and Article 8 minimum-year substitution are encoded from supplied career inputs. Remaining gaps are career-history reconstruction and proof, mixed-regime and least-advantageous-day elimination, delegated GRAPA exclusions and exemption amounts, detailed survivor eligibility, public-sector/self-employed pension formulas, foreign coordination, and complementary-pension taxation.

Flemish base immovable-withholding rates and core family/disability/modest-dwelling reductions are encoded, CIR 1992 base rates plus article-257 reductions, article-258 designated-dwelling/professional-use reduction-base mechanics, and article-259 rent-deductible pass-through for disability/family reductions are encoded for the non-Flemish Brussels/Walloon base layer, the Brussels BE HOME annual ownership-access premium is encoded for household real-right/domicile/notice-recipient eligibility, CPI indexation, euro rounding, and a property-tax notice deduction interface, the CIR 1992 article-464/1 additional-centimes interface is encoded when local centime counts are supplied, and a Belgian-facing routing bridge selects Flanders or Brussels/Wallonia with supplied non-Flemish centimes. Remaining gaps are province/municipality/agglomeration rate-table ingestion, Flemish local surcharges, Brussels/Walloon-specific exemptions and administrative rules, article-15 remissions, cadastral-income interfaces, and broader household proof predicates.

Worker article-17 component contribution rates and uncapped worker/employer contribution amounts are encoded; the ordinary Article 38 worker contribution bridge applies the 13.07 percent statutory employee rate to a supplied contribution base and supplied reductions; selected Article 19 remuneration-base inclusions and exclusions are encoded for ONVA holiday pay, old fixed mobility indemnities, bicycle/kilometric annual excesses, gifts, employee discounts, and copyright compensation; the 1994 law Article 108 annual special social-security contribution, Article 109 monthly withholding, Article 110 settlement/refund threshold, Article 68 pension-solidarity monthly withholding and complementary-capital withholding, 1994 health-insurance law Article 191 pension health-insurance withholding, and RD No. 33 invalidity/prepension floor-limited withholding are encoded; the 2019 mobility-budget Article 8/22 cash-balance contribution is encoded; the 1981 law Article 38/39/39ter/39quater Chapter 10 surfaces are encoded for double-holiday-pay withholding, public-sector vacation/Copernic/restructuring equalization withholding, profit-premium, second-pillar 8.86%, Wijninckx, company-car solidarity, economic-unemployment, non-recurring-result-benefit, and local public union-premium supplied-amount collection; Royal Decree No. 38 Articles 11, 12, 12bis, 13, 13bis, and 14 core self-employed contribution bases/categories are encoded with supplied index factor; and the Flemish Social Protection 2026 annual premium is encoded. Remaining gaps are full Article 19 remuneration-base coverage, current ONSS effective tables, wage caps, work-bonus calculation and low-wage reductions, additional Article 38 special contributions not yet modeled, delegated/indexed rates, caps, and floor revaluation where not supplied, self-employed administration/waivers/provisional-reduction procedures, remaining non-labour allocation/refund and capital-conversion surfaces, and full household-level integration.

VAT headline rates and VAT amount calculations are encoded when the rate category is supplied, selected household consumption category mapping is encoded for common Table A/B/C categories, Article 41/44 exemption override handling is encoded when the exemption predicate is supplied, and selected excise rates are encoded. Remaining gaps are detailed VAT product/exemption classification, full household consumption classification, excise category mapping, and statutory temporary measures.

Selected regional property/wealth-transfer tax rate surfaces are encoded, including Brussels/Flemish/Walloon inheritance and gift rate scales with Belgian-facing routing and Walloon future 2028 transfer-tax tables, Brussels article-131bis movable-gift exemption handling, Brussels/Walloon movable-gift donor-death condition or term exclusions, Flemish immovable withholding, CIR 1992 non-Flemish immovable-withholding base reductions with article-258 reduction-base mechanics and article-259 rent pass-through, Brussels BE HOME ownership-access premium amount/eligibility/deduction interface, and Belgian-facing immovable-withholding routing with supplied non-Flemish centimes. Federal securities-account tax, local centime tables, Flemish local surcharges, other exemptions, broader balance-sheet inputs, and household asset valuation remain.

Brussels rent allowance and relocation-accompaniment household gates, base amounts, child increments, rent caps, duration surfaces, and moving grant are encoded. Brussels social-housing rental is now encoded for social, moderate, and medium admission income bands; priority-title arithmetic; adapted-bedroom comparison and mutation eligibility; rental guarantee bounds; the Article 58/59/61 annual and monthly rent formula with floors and income caps; over-adapted and energy rent complements; selected lease-duration protections; and the Article 57 adapted-move rent reduction. Remaining gaps are exact current Article 30 index-factor tables and SLRB circular parameters, person-level child-bedroom pairing from sex/age/disability arrays, documentary proof and register administration, other Brussels housing-aid powers, and executable household-level oracle parity.

Brussels annual circulation tax and detailed current 1 July 2026 BIV tables are encoded; Flemish core passenger-vehicle circulation tax, additional LPG tax, municipal decime, post-2020 BIV formula, BIV guardrails, and electric/hydrogen BIV amount are encoded; Walloon current 2026-2027 passenger-car TMC, annual circulation tax, and additional LPG circulation tax are encoded; Belgian-facing passenger-car annual/entry-tax routing is encoded; the federal employee company-car benefit-in-kind formula is encoded for income year 2026; and the employer-side company-car CO2 solidarity contribution is encoded in the social-security special-contributions module. Remaining gaps are Brussels SmartMove, employer-side corporate-tax company-car disallowance and VAT surfaces, heavy/commercial and non-passenger vehicle routing, detailed exemptions, and household vehicle ownership inputs.

Article 6 taxable-income aggregation across the four net-income categories, Article 23 professional-income category aggregation and loss netting, Article 49/51 actual-or-forfait professional-expense selection, Article 86 assisting-spouse remuneration attribution, Articles 87/88 spouse professional-income imputation under joint assessment with an Article 89 separately taxed income exclusion interface, Article 126 joint-assessment gating, Article 127 joint-assessment income allocation, Article 129 supplied eligible loss-balance transfer bridge, Article 130 base-tax calculation, Article 134 tax-free-amount reduction, Article 134 paragraph 4 joint tax-free-amount allocation through spouse supplement assignment, excess transfer, and joint refundable-credit cap allocation, Article 134 refundable child-credit cap and net federal tax bridge, Articles 465-468 communal/agglomeration additional-tax mechanics, a federal-plus-local final-tax bridge with supplied withholding/prepayment settlement, an Article 6-to-final-tax liability pipeline with supplied local rates and withholding, selected federal Article 145 reductions, Article 147 replacement-income reductions and phaseout interfaces, and Articles 289bis/289ter/289ter/1 personal credits are encoded. Remaining gaps are delegated Article 129 loss-transfer modalities beyond supplied eligible balances, special professional-income inclusions and exemptions, special category-base rules, regional reductions/credits, final imputation ordering, local-rate table ingestion, professional withholding table calculation, partial-year company-car prorating, and end-to-end household disposable income.

parity_assessment.reason: The current RuleSpec now covers selected federal PIT including Article 6 taxable-income aggregation, Article 17/18 movable-income category aggregation, Article 23 professional-income netting, Article 49/51 actual-or-forfait professional-expense selection, Article 86 assisting-spouse professional-income attribution, Articles 87/88 joint-assessment spouse professional-income imputation with Article 89 separately taxed income excluded from the imputation base, Article 126 joint-assessment gating, Article 127 joint-assessment income allocation, an Article 129 supplied eligible loss-balance transfer bridge, Article 130 base tax, Article 134 tax-free-amount reduction, Article 134 paragraph 4 joint tax-free-amount allocation through spouse supplement assignment, excess transfer, and joint refundable-credit cap allocation, and refundable child-credit bridge, Articles 145/2, 145/7, 145/8, 145/31 through 145/35, 145/48, and 145/49 selected federal tax reductions, Articles 147, 151, 151/1, and 152 replacement-income reductions and phaseout interfaces, Articles 289bis, 289ter, and 289ter/1 personal credits, Articles 465-468 communal/agglomeration additional PIT mechanics, movable-withholding and regulated savings-deposit calculations, a final federal-plus-local PIT bridge with supplied withholding/prepayment settlement, an Article 6-to-final-tax liability pipeline with supplied local rates and withholding, and the employee company-car benefit-in-kind formula; worker social-security component rates, ordinary Article 38 employee-contribution bridge with supplied reductions, selected Article 19 remuneration-base mechanics, the 1994 law special social-security contribution, Article 68 pension-solidarity non-labour contribution, Article 191 pension health-insurance withholding, and RD No. 33 invalidity/prepension withholding, the 2019 mobility-budget cash-balance contribution, 1981 law Article 38/39/39ter/39quater Chapter 10 surfaces including employer company-car CO2 solidarity, Royal Decree No. 38 core self-employed contributions, and uncapped contribution amounts; worker retirement-pension age, current early-retirement thresholds, Article 5 retirement fractions, Article 7 survivor-pension interfaces, Article 7bis transition allowance, and Article 8 minimum-year substitution from supplied career inputs; the Flemish Social Protection premium; social-integration Article 3/4/7 eligibility, Article 14 amount selection, Article 15 supplied-index-factor bridge, Article 14 section 2 payable amount bridge, resource-exclusion, earned-income disregard, Article 24 business-transfer treatment, property/rent, movable-capital, transfer-of-assets, in-kind housing support, and cohabitant resource-attribution mechanics; GRAPA amount, resource-offset, and payable-amount mechanics; federal disability statutory amount/category surfaces; Brussels APA household-level eligibility, income/resource, annual/monthly award, minimum-payment, and suspension mechanics; Article 32 item 3/5 and Article 86/112 health-insurance holder-status bridging, Article 131 thirty-day continuity, primary-incapacity and invalidity rate/minimum interfaces, health-insurance waiting-stage and continuing-entitlement numeric predicates, unemployment Article 30/33 admission thresholds, Article 110 household-status classification, and Article 111/114/116/115/131ter benefit-rate, extension, minimum, and ordinary/temporary payable-amount surfaces, VAT rate, selected household category amount, Article 41/44 exemption override, excise, maternity-period surfaces, and Article 216/217/219bis/222 maternity-indemnity rate interfaces; Brussels/Flemish/Walloon family-benefit amount and core child-entitlement surfaces with Belgian-facing routing, German-speaking Community core 2019 amount, 2025-2027 indexation-freeze, 2025 annual-supplement override, and base-entitlement surfaces with the same routing bridge and supplied current/pre-2025 index-factor interfaces, federal guaranteed-family-benefits eligibility, resource-ceiling, claim-window, public-placement, birth/adoption, advance, and supplied amount interfaces, and the LGAF coordinated-law rank/monthly supplement/age/orphan/birth/adoption/indexation amount schedule; Brussels rent allowance and relocation-accompaniment household gates, base amounts, child increments, rent caps, duration surfaces, moving grant, and Brussels social-housing rental admission, priority, adapted-bedroom, rental-guarantee, rent-formula, complement, and lease-review surfaces; Brussels/Flemish/Walloon current core passenger vehicle-tax surfaces with Belgian-facing annual/entry-tax routing; Flemish immovable-withholding, CIR 1992 non-Flemish immovable-withholding base rates/reductions including article-258 designated-dwelling and professional-use reduction-base mechanics, article-259 rent pass-through, Brussels BE HOME ownership-access premium eligibility, CPI indexation, euro rounding, and notice deduction interface, and the additional-centimes interface; and main Brussels/Flemish/Walloon inheritance/gift-tax rate schedules with Belgian-facing regional routing, Walloon future 2028 transfer-tax tables, Brussels article-131bis movable-gift exemption handling, and Brussels/Walloon movable-gift donor-death condition or term exclusions. It still lacks regional/local property-tax rate-table ingestion, many exemptions/special regimes, pension career-history reconstruction and public-sector/self-employed pension formulas, additional Article 38 special contributions, delegated ONSS effective tables and work-bonus calculations, self-employed administration/waivers/provisional-reduction procedures, remaining non-labour allocation/refund and capital-conversion surfaces, remaining PIT delegated Article 129 modalities, special category-base rules, regional reductions/credits and final imputation ordering, delegated Brussels APA current index-factor tables and medical-guide evidence scoring, remaining Brussels housing supports/current index factors/SLRB circular parameters and administrative proof, Brussels SmartMove, employer-side company-car corporate-tax/VAT surfaces, unemployment full employment-history reconstruction, delegated household-status definitions/proof, sanction and activation details, remaining health-insurance broader Article 32 status proof, Article 131 date reconstruction, and contribution-minimum proof, third-and-later LGAF birth-grant BEF-to-EUR conversion/source completion, German-speaking Community exact current-index value/table ingestion, delegated regional family-benefit proof/obstacle/education conditions, delegated current social-integration index-factor source, and household-level oracle parity needed to match BEAMM's documented scope.
next_highest_value_batches: Brussels and Walloon real-property-tax local-rate tables, exemptions, article-15 remissions, cadastral-income interfaces, and administrative predicates
Flemish/Brussels/Walloon inheritance and gift-tax exemptions, reduced rates, estate/transaction predicates, and household beneficiary/transaction inputs
Brussels SmartMove, employer-side company-car corporate-tax/VAT rules, and household vehicle ownership interfaces
remaining non-labour-income allocation/refund and capital-conversion contribution surfaces plus delegated ONSS current tables/reductions
remaining sickness/maternity broader Article 32 status proof, Article 131 date reconstruction, wage-history proof, contribution-minimum proof, and agency indexation tables
Brussels APA current index-factor tables, medical-guide evidence scoring, regional/community care-support expansion, and executable household-level oracle cases
remaining Brussels housing current index factors, SLRB circular parameters, other housing-aid powers, person-level bedroom matching, and administrative proof surfaces
remaining PIT special category-base rules, regional reductions/credits, final imputation ordering, professional withholding tables, and local-rate table ingestion
worker-pension career-history reconstruction, mixed-regime career selection, public-sector/self-employed pensions, and detailed survivor eligibility
LGAF third-and-later birth-grant conversion/source completion plus German-speaking Community exact current-index values/table ingestion and delegated regional family-benefit proof completion
household-level oracle pinning for BELMOD, EUROMOD Belgium, or FANTASI where executable access exists, or for OpenFisca Belgium only if a real Belgium country package/API is found
